_comment: OQL field whitelist. The validator in oql.py allows fields that either (a) start with one of `field_prefixes` (followed by `.` or end of string), (b) match an entry in `exact_fields`, or (c) are not in `forbidden_fields`. Expand as your grid surfaces new fields. Original list from ECS spec (https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/ecs/current/index.html) + SO/Zeek/Suricata fields at docs.securityonion.net. Additional field families (connection, dhcp, ... wireguard) cover SO/Zeek/Suricata fields a live SO 3.0 grid surfaces beyond the base ECS set.
_comment_ecs_zeek: Modern SO / Elastic-Agent 9.x populates ECS field names for Zeek data (dns.query.name, client.bytes, hash.ja3s, http.virtual_host, ...) while the zeek.* fields are mapped-but-empty. These ECS names are already covered by the prefixes above (dns/client/server/network/connection/hash/ssl/http/user_agent/file) and so already pass is_allowed(); they are listed explicitly here too as the documented, intended OQL surface for the agent so the field-resolution layer in so_client/fields.py and the OQL allowlist agree on the same canonical names. The zeek.* prefix stays valid for older SO and synth fixtures.
